name: Masha's Easter Kulich
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Melted butter or canola oil
recipeInstructions:
In a large mixing bowl, combine milk, yeast and honey; stir well. Cover the bowl with a clean dish towel and set aside in a warm place for 10 minutes or until the yeast foams.
In a smaller bowl, soak raisins and saffron in rum for 10 minutes.
Add the raisin mixture and enough flour to the yeast so it forms a thick batter. Stir vigorously with a wooden spoon about 20 times. Cover the bowl again and let the dough rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
In a clean bowl, add salt, vanilla, eggs, butter, almonds, fruits, and enough additional flour to make a kneadable dough. Transfer the dough to a floured board or counter; knead for 10 minutes until elastic and silky to touch.
Clean the bowl. Place the kneaded dough into the bowl, cover, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour. Punch down the dough, then let it rise again for 25 minutes.
Wash and dry an empty 3-pound coffee can. Line the can with unprinted buttered brown paper (such as from a grocery bag), cutting into long strips, allowing the tops of the strips to hang over the top of the can. Tie the ends against the side of the can with string. Overhanging paper strips make it easier to remove the baked bread from the can.
Roll the dough into a ball and press into the can. Cover with a clean dish towel and let rise for 30 minutes, or until the dough rises above the top of the can.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Bake the bread in the can on the lowest oven shelf for 30 minutes. Reduce heat to 350°F and bake an additional 40 minutes, or until golden brown. Let cool for 10 minutes, then open the bottom of the can with a can opener and remove the bread by lifting out with paper strips.
Note: Russian cooks often ice their kulich with a mixture of egg white (or equivalent egg white powder and water), a pinch of salt, 1/2 cup powdered sugar or warmed honey, and 1/4 teaspoon almond extract. Beat the egg white until stiff peaks form, then fold in the remaining ingredients; drizzle over the kulich. Yield: 1 kulich.
